title: sambucus nigra var. cerulea
intro: Sambucus nigra var. cerulea, also known as the blue elderberry, is a variety of the black elder tree native to Europe and North Africa. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antimicrobial properties. The blue elderberry has numerous health benefits, including reducing the severity of cold and flu symptoms, lowering cholesterol levels, and providing antioxidant protection. The herbal preparations of the blue elderberry include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
